title: TsuzuLint configuration
description: Schema for .tzlintrc and .tzlintrc.{json,jsonc,yaml,yml} files. This is the JSON-level contract; YAML-only leniency (yes/no/on/off booleans, comments, a leading BOM) and the empty-file-means-default behavior are intentionally not modeled here.

language.description: Document language tag (e.g. "ja"). null or absent leaves it unset.

message-language.description: Diagnostic-message locale, independent of the document language.

extends.description: Preset(s) whose rule settings form a base layer under this file's own `rules` (this file wins on overlap; later array entries win over earlier). A preset id, an array of ids, or null/absent for none.

rules.description: Map of rule id to its setting. Rule ids are arbitrary strings; they are not validated against a known-rule set yet (that arrives with the rules crate).

formats.description: Per-input-format settings, keyed by format id. Only `csv` and `tsv` are wired (an unknown id is rejected by the loader at resolve time — not expressible here, so this allows arbitrary keys). Each value describes how to extract and lint that format's columns.

format.description: One delimited format's settings: whether the first row is a header, an optional single-character delimiter override, and the target columns.

header.description: Whether the first record is a header row (enables selecting columns by name).

delimiter.description: A single-character delimiter override (else the format default: `,` for csv, tab for tsv). Must be one character and, at resolve time, ASCII (the non-ASCII check is the loader's, not expressible here).

columns.description: Target columns to lint, keyed by selector: a 1-based column number (e.g. "2") or a header name (requires `header: true`). Keys are arbitrary strings (the name-vs-number resolution and the header requirement are the loader's, not expressible here).

column.description: One target column: how to parse its cells and an optional rule overlay layered over the base `rules` (the column wins).

parse-mode.description: How each cell is parsed before linting. Defaults to markdown.

ruleSetting.description: false disables the rule; true enables it with defaults; an object overrides its severity and/or passes rule-specific options.

options.description: Rule-specific options, passed through verbatim. Any JSON value is accepted.
